message:
fix(session,scheduler): harden active path traversal against dangling references and fix timer count in debug output

session_store_v2.rs — active path dangling-reference detection:
- read_active_path() previously broke out of the traversal loop
  silently when a parent_entry_id was not found in the index or when
  a frame referenced by the index was missing from disk. This could
  silently truncate session history to just the root frame.
- Now, if frames have already been collected (i.e., we're mid-chain,
  not at the start), a missing parent or missing frame returns an
  explicit error: "missing parent entry detected while reading active
  path" / "index references missing frame while reading active path"
- The start-of-chain case (frames.is_empty()) still breaks cleanly,
  since the root frame having no parent is expected behavior

tests/session_store_v2.rs:
- Add read_active_path_errors_on_missing_parent_reference test that
  corrupts a segment to set parent_entry_id="Z" on the second frame,
  then asserts read_active_path("B") fails with the expected error

scheduler.rs — debug timer count accuracy:
- Scheduler::fmt::Debug previously reported timer_heap.len() which
  includes cancelled timers still sitting in the heap. Replace with
  timer_count() which subtracts cancelled timers for an accurate
  count of live timers.
- Add scheduler_debug_reports_live_timer_count test: creates 2 timers,
  cancels one, and asserts debug output shows timer_count: 1 and
  cancelled_timers: 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
